title: Project Constitution Front-Matter
description: YAML front-matter schema for .claude/project-constitution.md. Locks each project's tooling profile (shell, package manager, language runtime, canonical commands, forbidden patterns, bypass patterns) so dispatched workers, hooks, and skills observe consistent conventions. See docs/reference/project-constitution.md for narrative coverage and loom-vin / loom-6f8 for lineage.

shell.description: Project shell envelope. Commands defined elsewhere in this file are expected to run inside this envelope. Use empty strings when the project has no shell wrapper (plain host shell).

enter.description: Command an interactive operator runs once to drop into the project shell. Examples: 'devbox shell', 'nix-shell', 'poetry shell'. Empty string when no shell wrapper is used.

run_prefix.description: Prefix prepended to commands when invoking them non-interactively (CI, hooks, scripts). Examples: 'devbox run', 'poetry run', 'nix-shell --run'. Empty string when no prefix is needed.

package_manager.description: The single package manager that owns dependency resolution for this project. Mixing managers (e.g. pnpm and npm, uv and pip) is the canonical failure this field guards against.

language.description: Primary language runtime for the project. Use 'polyglot' when no one runtime dominates and 'unknown' when the runtime has not yet been pinned.

version.description: Semver-ish version pin (e.g. '3.13', '1.24', '20.11.0'). May be empty when language.runtime is 'bash', 'polyglot', or 'unknown'.

forbidden.description: Bash command patterns the agent must never run inside this project. Typically used to lock in the package_manager choice (e.g. 'npm install' on a pnpm-only project) or to forbid known-destructive operations. Patterns are matched as substrings of the bash invocation.

canonical_commands.description: The agreed command for each workflow verb. Skills, hooks, and dispatched workers read these instead of guessing. Empty string indicates the project has no such verb (e.g. lint='' on a project with no linter). NOTE: 'deploy' is the canonical home for the deploy step as of loom-oxs.3; the legacy 'workflow.json .deploy' hint is DEPRECATED in favour of canonical_commands.deploy. Migrate an existing 'workflow.json .deploy' value to canonical_commands.deploy (its default impl is 'script/deploy').

build.description: Build command. Multi-step builds may be a single line with '&&' or '; '.

test.description: Test command. Should run unit tests at minimum; integration suites typically live in a separate verb or as flagged variants of this command.

lint.description: Lint command. Empty string when the project has no linter.

gen.description: Code-generation command (e.g. './scripts/gen' for Goa projects). Empty string when there is no generation step.

dev.description: Dev-server / watch command. Empty string when not applicable (libraries, CLIs).

deploy.description: Deploy command. Default impl is 'script/deploy' under the GitHub-style script/ convention (loom-oxs). Empty string when the project has no deploy step. Supersedes the DEPRECATED 'workflow.json .deploy' hint (loom-oxs.3): migrate an existing 'workflow.json .deploy' value here. Optional — older constitutions predating loom-oxs.3 may omit this key.

bypass_patterns.description: Bash command patterns that should bypass constitution enforcement. Escape hatches for legitimate edge cases (e.g. running an ad-hoc 'python3 -c ...' on a uv project for diagnostics). Use sparingly — every bypass widens the agent's blast radius.

invariants.description: Project-specific ARCHITECTURAL invariants enforced by the constitution-enforce hook across Bash AND the write-class tools (Edit/Write/MultiEdit) — distinct from the argv-shaped tooling rules (forbidden/package_manager/run_prefix), which are Bash-only. Each entry declares which tools it applies to, a regex deny_pattern matched against the tool's relevant input (Bash → .command; write-class → .file_path + .content/.new_string), and a message shown on a block. Example: forbid direct file I/O on a 'touch the world only through MCP' project. Optional — older constitutions predating loom-z3m.14 may omit this key. A match → the hook exits 2 with the message; non-matches and any parse/compile uncertainty fail open (exit 0).

id.description: Short stable identifier for the invariant (e.g. 'no-direct-file-io'). Shown in the block message so the operator can find the rule.

applies_to.description: Which tool calls this invariant is checked against. An invariant only fires for a tool listed here.

deny_pattern.description: A Python-flavored regex (re.search). Matched against the tool's relevant input: Bash → the .command string; Edit/Write/MultiEdit → the .file_path joined with the written body (.content for Write, .new_string for Edit, every edit's .new_string for MultiEdit). A search hit blocks the call. An uncompilable pattern fails open (never blocks).

message.description: Human-readable explanation shown when the invariant blocks a call. State the rule and the sanctioned alternative (e.g. 'Touch the world only through MCP — direct file I/O is forbidden').
